metadata.description: Temporal lag decorator for HRF functions

semantic_description.purpose: Validate the lag_hrf function that applies temporal lag to existing HRF objects
semantic_description.algorithm:
The lag_hrf function is a decorator that applies a temporal lag to an existing HRF object. This creates a time-shifted version of the hemodynamic response, which is useful for modeling delayed responses or temporal variations in neural processing.
Mathematical behavior: - Time shifting: f_lagged(t) = f_original(t - lag) - Positive lag shifts response later in time (delayed response) - Negative lag shifts response earlier in time (anticipated response) - Preserves shape: only temporal position changes - Span adjustment: span_new = span_original + max(0, lag)
semantic_description.mathematical_properties: Key properties to validate: 1. Time shift accuracy: exact temporal displacement 2. Shape preservation: response magnitude unchanged at corresponding times 3. Span adjustment: proper domain extension for positive lags 4. Attribute inheritance: nbasis, name modification, parameter tracking 5. Edge case handling: zero lag, negative lag, large lag values 6. Mathematical equivalence: lag_hrf(h, L)(t) ≡ h(t - L)
semantic_description.edge_cases: - Zero lag: should produce identical output to original - Negative lag: shifts response earlier, span unchanged - Large lag: significant span increase, delayed response - Multi-basis HRFs: preserve basis count and matrix structure

input.name: search_times
input.description: Time points for peak timing validation

implementations.R:
# Create base HRFs for testing hrf_spmg1_obj <- as_hrf(hrf_spmg1, name = "spmg1", nbasis = 1, span = 30) hrf_gaussian_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gaussian(t, mean = 6, sd = 2), name = "gaussian", nbasis = 1, span = 30) hrf_gamma_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_gamma(t, shape = 6, rate = 1), name = "gamma", nbasis = 1, span = 30) hrf_bspline_obj <- as_hrf(function(t) hrf_bspline(t, N = 4), name = "bspline", nbasis = 4, span = 24)
# Input vectors eval_times <- c(0.0, 2.0, 5.0, 8.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0) comparison_times <- c(-3.0, -1.0, 2.0, 5.0, 7.0, 12.0, 17.0) search_times <- c(8.0, 9.0, 10.0, 11.0, 12.0)
# Test positive lag shift lagged_spmg1 <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 3.0) lagged_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1, eval_times) equivalence_check <- evaluate(hrf_spmg1_obj, comparison_times) span_adjustment <- attr(lagged_spmg1, "span") nbasis_preserved <- nbasis(lagged_spmg1) name_modification <- attr(lagged_spmg1, "name")
# Test negative lag shift lagged_gaussian_neg <- lag_hrf(hrf_gaussian_obj, -2.0) negative_lag_values <- evaluate(lagged_gaussian_neg, eval_times) span_no_increase <- attr(lagged_gaussian_neg, "span") name_negative_lag <- attr(lagged_gaussian_neg, "name")
# Test zero lag identity lagged_gamma_zero <- lag_hrf(hrf_gamma_obj, 0.0) identity_values <- evaluate(lagged_gamma_zero, eval_times) span_unchanged <- attr(lagged_gamma_zero, "span") name_zero_lag <- attr(lagged_gamma_zero, "name")
# Test large lag boundary lagged_spmg1_large <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 25.0) large_lag_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1_large, eval_times) large_span_increase <- attr(lagged_spmg1_large, "span")
# Test peak timing shift lagged_spmg1_peak <- lag_hrf(hrf_spmg1_obj, 4.0) peak_values <- evaluate(lagged_spmg1_peak, search_times) peak_shifted_correctly <- which.max(peak_values) == which.max(evaluate(hrf_spmg1_obj, search_times - 4.0)) peak_magnitude_preserved <- max(peak_values)
# Test multi-basis preservation lagged_bspline <- lag_hrf(hrf_bspline_obj, 2.5) multi_basis_result <- evaluate(lagged_bspline, eval_times) multi_basis_shape <- dim(multi_basis_result) nbasis_multi_preserved <- nbasis(lagged_bspline) span_multi_adjusted <- attr(lagged_bspline, "span")
implementations.Python: # Python implementation using closure pattern # Create a function that returns a closure capturing the lag value. # Use functools.wraps or similar for metadata preservation. # Ensure span calculation handles max(0, lag) correctly. # Implementation pending - refer to R reference for mathematical behavior
